Fixed Intercept and Interaction Models

Maya Arnott

1. Data Prepration

Libraries and load in our data

Prepare the variables

asthma_precip_pm25 <- asthma_precip_pm25 |>
  mutate(
    date        = as.Date(date),
    borough     = as.factor(borough),
    year        = as.factor(year(date)),
    day_of_week = as.factor(wday(date, label = TRUE)),
    season      = as.factor(case_when(
      month(date) %in% c(12, 1, 2)  ~ "Winter",
      month(date) %in% c(3, 4, 5)   ~ "Spring",
      month(date) %in% c(6, 7, 8)   ~ "Summer",
      month(date) %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
    ))
  )

# create log transformed ppt and pm25 var
asthma_precip_pm25 <- asthma_precip_pm25 |> 
  mutate(
    log_ppt = log(ppt + 1)
  )

asthma_precip_pm25 <- asthma_precip_pm25 |> 
  mutate(
    pm25 = if_else(pm25 < 0, 0, pm25),
    log_pm25 = log(pm25 + 1)
  )

# create thresholds for ppt
asthma_precip_pm25 <- asthma_precip_pm25 |>
  mutate(ppt_cat = case_when(
    ppt == 0        ~ "none",
    ppt < 1         ~ "trace",
    ppt < 10        ~ "light",
    ppt < 25        ~ "moderate",
    ppt >= 25       ~ "heavy"
  ) |> factor(levels = c("none", "trace", "light", "moderate", "heavy")))

I created an offset population variable because the raw counts can be misleading. I used data from thetidycensus package to do this.

census_api_key(Sys.getenv("CENSUS_API_KEY"), overwrite = TRUE)
## To install your API key for use in future sessions, run this function with `install = TRUE`.
# pop for years 2016 to 2019
years <- c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019)

borough_pop <- purrr::map_dfr(years, ~{
  get_acs(
    geography = "county",
    variables = "B01003_001",  # total population estimate
    state     = "NY",
    county    = c("New York", "Kings", "Queens", "Bronx", "Richmond"),
    year      = .x
  ) |>
    mutate(year = as.character(.x))
}) |>
  mutate(borough = case_when(
    grepl("New York County", NAME)  ~ "Manhattan",
    grepl("Kings County", NAME)     ~ "Brooklyn",
    grepl("Queens County", NAME)    ~ "Queens",
    grepl("Bronx County", NAME)     ~ "Bronx",
    grepl("Richmond County", NAME)  ~ "Staten Island"
  )) |>
  select(borough, year, population = estimate)
## Getting data from the 2012-2016 5-year ACS
## Getting data from the 2013-2017 5-year ACS
## Getting data from the 2014-2018 5-year ACS
## Getting data from the 2015-2019 5-year ACS
# merge with other data
asthma_precip_pm25 <- asthma_precip_pm25 |>
  left_join(borough_pop, by = c("borough", "year"))

Temperature is likely a confounder in this relationship, and should be added to the model. I am assuming that the temperature across boroughs is fairly similar, and therefore only using data from one weather station in Central Park for average temperature will suffice.

# will add and merge daily average temperature data to csv file

Scale variables

I scaled log_ppt, log_pm25, and temperature because this helps mixed models converge and makes coefficients across covariates comparable.

# scale variables 
asthma_precip_pm25 <- asthma_precip_pm25 |>
  mutate(
    log_ppt_scaled = as.numeric(scale(log_ppt)),
    log_pm25_scaled    = as.numeric(scale(log_pm25))
    # add temperature to scale
    )

2. Negative Binomial Model over Poisson

# poisson
pois.model <- glm(
  asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled + borough + season +
    day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)),
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = asthma_precip_pm25
)

dispersiontest(pois.model)
## 
##  Overdispersion test
## 
## data:  pois.model
## z = 25.709, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true dispersion is greater than 1
## sample estimates:
## dispersion 
##   2.198475

According to the overdispersion test, there is evidence of significant overdispersion (dispersion = 2.20, p<.001), which justifies the use of a negative binomial model.

3. ICC

# how much do daily visits cluster within borough?
asthma_precip_pm25 <- asthma_precip_pm25 |> 
  mutate(asthma_rate = (asthma_er_count / population) * 100000)

asthma.ICC <- ICCest(borough, asthma_rate, data = asthma_precip_pm25)
## Warning in ICCest(borough, asthma_rate, data = asthma_precip_pm25): 'x' has
## been coerced to a factor
asthma.ICC$ICC
## [1] 0.6802938
asthma.ICC$LowerCI
## [1] 0.4327528
asthma.ICC$UpperCI
## [1] 0.946171
# how much does rain cluster within borough?
ppt.ICC <- ICCest(borough, log_ppt_scaled, data = asthma_precip_pm25)
## Warning in ICCest(borough, log_ppt_scaled, data = asthma_precip_pm25): 'x' has
## been coerced to a factor
ppt.ICC$ICC
## [1] -0.0006795941

ICC Results Table

icc_table <- data.frame(
  Variable      = c("Asthma ED Visit Rate (per 100,000)", "Log Precipitation (scaled)"),
  ICC           = c(round(asthma.ICC$ICC, 3), round(ppt.ICC$ICC, 3)),
  Lower_CI      = c(round(asthma.ICC$LowerCI, 3), NA),
  Upper_CI      = c(round(asthma.ICC$UpperCI, 3), NA),
  Interpretation = c(
    "68% of variation is between boroughs",
    "No borough-level clustering"
  )
)

icc_table |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(
    Variable       = "Variable",
    ICC            = "ICC",
    Lower_CI       = "95% CI Lower",
    Upper_CI       = "95% CI Upper",
    Interpretation = "Interpretation"
  ) |>
  tab_header(title = "Table 1. Intraclass Correlation Coefficients by Borough")
Table 1. Intraclass Correlation Coefficients by Borough
Variable ICC 95% CI Lower 95% CI Upper Interpretation
Asthma ED Visit Rate (per 100,000) 0.680 0.433 0.946 68% of variation is between boroughs
Log Precipitation (scaled) -0.001 NA NA No borough-level clustering

Asthma Rate ICC = 0.68. 68% of the total variation in daily asthma ED visit rates is attributable to between-borough differences, whereas only 32% is day-to-day within-borough variation. This justifies including borough fixed intercepts.

Precipitation ICC = -0.001. This means that precipitation is not confounded by borough and the variation in rain is only temporal.

4. Between borough model (ecological)

dta_btwn <- asthma_precip_pm25 |> 
  group_by(borough) |> 
  summarize(
    mean_asthma_rate = mean(asthma_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_log_ppt     = mean(log_ppt_scaled, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_pm25        = mean(log_pm25_scaled, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mod.btwn <- lm(
  mean_asthma_rate ~ mean_log_ppt + mean_pm25,
  data = dta_btwn
)

summary(mod.btwn)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = mean_asthma_rate ~ mean_log_ppt + mean_pm25, data = dta_btwn)
## 
## Residuals:
##       1       2       3       4       5 
##  0.3140  0.8215  0.5000 -0.9162 -0.7193 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)    3.1741     0.4879   6.506   0.0228 *
## mean_log_ppt 420.8735   224.8409   1.872   0.2021  
## mean_pm25     -6.3840     4.1763  -1.529   0.2660  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 1.091 on 2 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.6534, Adjusted R-squared:  0.3069 
## F-statistic: 1.886 on 2 and 2 DF,  p-value: 0.3466

With 2 degrees of freedom, the model has very low statistical power, which justifies the use of a fixed intercept model.

5. Main Model: Borough Fixed Intercepts (no interactions)

mod.fixed <- glm.nb(
  asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled + borough + 
    season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)),
  data = asthma_precip_pm25
)

# model outputs
summary(mod.fixed)
## 
## Call:
## glm.nb(formula = asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled + 
##     borough + season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)), 
##     data = asthma_precip_pm25, init.theta = 44.49253143, link = log)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                        Estimate Std. Error   z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)          -9.8004451  0.0079334 -1235.339  < 2e-16 ***
## log_ppt_scaled       -0.0074862  0.0026189    -2.859  0.00426 ** 
## log_pm25_scaled      -0.0011482  0.0027815    -0.413  0.67976    
## boroughBrooklyn      -0.3788347  0.0069579   -54.446  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughManhattan     -0.6080088  0.0075090   -80.970  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughQueens        -1.2017802  0.0077389  -155.291  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughStaten Island -0.5631593  0.0095165   -59.177  < 2e-16 ***
## seasonSpring          0.0222114  0.0069426     3.199  0.00138 ** 
## seasonSummer         -0.2454833  0.0072990   -33.632  < 2e-16 ***
## seasonWinter         -0.0424625  0.0074450    -5.704 1.17e-08 ***
## day_of_week.L        -0.1273817  0.0067000   -19.012  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week.Q        -0.1413710  0.0067328   -20.997  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week.C         0.0752343  0.0066446    11.323  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week^4        -0.0789737  0.0065890   -11.986  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week^5         0.0313904  0.0065856     4.767 1.87e-06 ***
## day_of_week^6        -0.0183063  0.0065923    -2.777  0.00549 ** 
## year2017             -0.0529233  0.0070806    -7.474 7.75e-14 ***
## year2018              0.0005848  0.0070441     0.083  0.93384    
## year2019             -0.0358654  0.0070997    -5.052 4.38e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for Negative Binomial(44.4925) family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 36373.5  on 7238  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  7567.5  on 7220  degrees of freedom
##   (26 observations deleted due to missingness)
## AIC: 53191
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 1
## 
## 
##               Theta:  44.49 
##           Std. Err.:  1.46 
## 
##  2 x log-likelihood:  -53151.14
beta_ppt <- summary(mod.fixed)$coefficients["log_ppt_scaled", "Estimate"]
se_ppt   <- summary(mod.fixed)$coefficients["log_ppt_scaled", "Std. Error"]
lci_ppt  <- beta_ppt - 1.96 * se_ppt
uci_ppt  <- beta_ppt + 1.96 * se_ppt

rr_ppt <- paste0(
  round(exp(beta_ppt), 3), " (95% CI: ",
  round(exp(lci_ppt), 3), ", ",
  round(exp(uci_ppt), 3), ")"
)
rr_ppt
## [1] "0.993 (95% CI: 0.987, 0.998)"

Main Model Results Table

mod.fixed |>
  tidy(conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) |>
  filter(!str_detect(term, "day_of_week")) |> 
  mutate(
    term = case_when(
      term == "(Intercept)"          ~ "Intercept",
      term == "log_ppt_scaled"       ~ "Log Precipitation (1 SD)",
      term == "log_pm25_scaled"      ~ "Log PM2.5 (1 SD)",
      term == "boroughBrooklyn"      ~ "Borough: Brooklyn vs Bronx",
      term == "boroughManhattan"     ~ "Borough: Manhattan vs Bronx",
      term == "boroughQueens"        ~ "Borough: Queens vs Bronx",
      term == "boroughStaten Island" ~ "Borough: Staten Island vs Bronx",
      term == "seasonSpring"         ~ "Season: Spring vs Fall",
      term == "seasonSummer"         ~ "Season: Summer vs Fall",
      term == "seasonWinter"         ~ "Season: Winter vs Fall",
      term == "year2017"             ~ "Year: 2017 vs 2016",
      term == "year2018"             ~ "Year: 2018 vs 2016",
      term == "year2019"             ~ "Year: 2019 vs 2016",
      TRUE ~ term
    )
  ) |>
  select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(
    term      = "Variable",
    estimate  = "Rate Ratio",
    conf.low  = "95% CI Lower",
    conf.high = "95% CI Upper",
    p.value   = "p-value"
  ) |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), decimals = 3) |>
  fmt(columns = p.value, fns = function(x) ifelse(x < 0.001, "<0.001", round(x, 3))) |>
  tab_header(title = "Table 2. Borough Fixed Intercept Negative Binomial Model") |>
  tab_footnote("Rate ratios and 95% CIs shown. Reference categories: Bronx (borough), Fall (season), 2016 (year). Offset = log(population).")
Table 2. Borough Fixed Intercept Negative Binomial Model
Variable Rate Ratio 95% CI Lower 95% CI Upper p-value
Intercept 0.000 0.000 0.000 <0.001
Log Precipitation (1 SD) 0.993 0.987 0.998 0.004
Log PM2.5 (1 SD) 0.999 0.993 1.004 0.68
Borough: Brooklyn vs Bronx 0.685 0.675 0.694 <0.001
Borough: Manhattan vs Bronx 0.544 0.536 0.553 <0.001
Borough: Queens vs Bronx 0.301 0.296 0.305 <0.001
Borough: Staten Island vs Bronx 0.569 0.559 0.580 <0.001
Season: Spring vs Fall 1.022 1.009 1.036 0.001
Season: Summer vs Fall 0.782 0.771 0.794 <0.001
Season: Winter vs Fall 0.958 0.945 0.973 <0.001
Year: 2017 vs 2016 0.948 0.935 0.962 <0.001
Year: 2018 vs 2016 1.001 0.987 1.014 0.934
Year: 2019 vs 2016 0.965 0.951 0.978 <0.001
Rate ratios and 95% CIs shown. Reference categories: Bronx (borough), Fall (season), 2016 (year). Offset = log(population).

Fixed Intercept Coefficient Plot

coef_plot_df <- mod.fixed |>
  tidy(conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = FALSE) |>  # keep on log scale for plot
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>
  filter(!str_detect(term, "day_of_week")) |> 
  mutate(term = case_when(
    term == "log_ppt_scaled"            ~ "Log Precipitation (1 SD)",
    term == "log_pm25_scaled"           ~ "Log PM2.5 (1 SD)",
    term == "boroughBrooklyn"           ~ "Borough: Brooklyn vs Bronx",
    term == "boroughManhattan"          ~ "Borough: Manhattan vs Bronx",
    term == "boroughQueens"             ~ "Borough: Queens vs Bronx",
    term == "boroughStaten Island"      ~ "Borough: Staten Island vs Bronx",
    term == "seasonSpring"              ~ "Season: Spring vs Fall",
    term == "seasonSummer"              ~ "Season: Summer vs Fall",
    term == "seasonWinter"              ~ "Season: Winter vs Fall",
    term == "year2017"                  ~ "Year: 2017 vs 2016",
    term == "year2018"                  ~ "Year: 2018 vs 2016",
    term == "year2019"                  ~ "Year: 2019 vs 2016",
    TRUE ~ term
  ))

ggplot(coef_plot_df, aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(term, estimate))) + 
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey55",
    linewidth  = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
    width     = 0.25,
    linewidth = 0.8,
    color     = "#3B528B"
  ) +
  geom_point(color = "#3B528B", size = 3) +
  labs(
    title    = "Fixed Intercept Negative Binomial Model Coefficients",
    subtitle = "± 95% confidence interval",
    x        = "Log Rate Ratio (effect on asthma ED visit rate)",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 9, color = "#555555", lineheight = 1.3, margin = margin(b = 8)),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) 
## Warning: `geom_errorbarh()` was deprecated in ggplot2 4.0.0.
## ℹ Please use the `orientation` argument of `geom_errorbar()` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

# save plot as png
ggsave(
  filename = "outputs/coef_plot_fixed_intercept.png",
  plot     = last_plot(),
  width    = 8,
  height   = 6,
  dpi      = 300
)

log_ppt_scaled A 1 standard deviation increase in log precipitation is associated with a 0.75% decrease in asthma ED visit rates, holding all other variables constant. This is statistically significant but indicates a very small effect on asthma ED visit rates.

log_pm25_scaled The p-value was not significant.

‘borough’ All boroughs had an association with asthma ED visits that was were highly significant. Bronx had the highest asthma ED visit rate, and all other boroughs were significantly lower.

‘season’ All seasons had an association with asthma ED visits that was were highly significant. The summer season was highly protective and the spring season showed a slightly elevated association with asthma ED visits, possibly due to an overlap with allergy season.

Marginal Effects plot

# include a marginal effects plot for mod.fixed

6. Effect Modification - Borough Interaction

# reduced model (as noted earlier)
mod.fixed <- glm.nb(
  asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled + borough + 
    season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)),
  data = asthma_precip_pm25
)

# full model with interaction
mod.full <- glm.nb(
  asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled * borough + log_pm25_scaled + 
    season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)),
  data = asthma_precip_pm25
)

# likelihood ratio test
lrtest(mod.fixed, mod.full)
## Likelihood ratio test
## 
## Model 1: asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled + borough + 
##     season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population))
## Model 2: asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled * borough + log_pm25_scaled + 
##     season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population))
##   #Df LogLik Df  Chisq Pr(>Chisq)
## 1  20 -26576                     
## 2  24 -26574  4 2.4625     0.6514
# anova test
Anova(mod.full, type = "III")
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type III tests)
## 
## Response: asthma_er_count
##                        LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## log_ppt_scaled              0.5  1     0.4832    
## borough                 25568.3  4     <2e-16 ***
## log_pm25_scaled             0.1  1     0.7018    
## season                   1662.1  3     <2e-16 ***
## day_of_week              1128.3  6     <2e-16 ***
## year                       85.3  3     <2e-16 ***
## log_ppt_scaled:borough      2.5  4     0.6513    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

The LRT p-value is 0.6492, which means that the interaction term does not significantly improve the model fit. Although we proved that boroughs have different baseline asthma rates, precipitation affects them similarly and the association does not differ significantly across boroughs.

7. Categorical Precipitation Sensitivity Analysis

mod.full <- glm.nb(
  asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + borough + log_pm25_scaled + 
    season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)),
  data = asthma_precip_pm25
)

# categorical ppt model
model.cat <- glm.nb(
  asthma_er_count ~ ppt_cat + borough + log_pm25_scaled + season + 
    day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)), 
  data = asthma_precip_pm25
)

# AIC
summary(model.cat)
## 
## Call:
## glm.nb(formula = asthma_er_count ~ ppt_cat + borough + log_pm25_scaled + 
##     season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)), data = asthma_precip_pm25, 
##     init.theta = 44.54069728, link = log)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                        Estimate Std. Error   z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)          -9.7966586  0.0081129 -1207.538  < 2e-16 ***
## ppt_cattrace          0.0063419  0.0072135     0.879  0.37931    
## ppt_catlight         -0.0157135  0.0068441    -2.296  0.02168 *  
## ppt_catmoderate      -0.0214872  0.0091882    -2.339  0.01936 *  
## ppt_catheavy         -0.0021781  0.0142620    -0.153  0.87862    
## boroughBrooklyn      -0.3787233  0.0069551   -54.452  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughManhattan     -0.6077331  0.0075095   -80.928  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughQueens        -1.2017605  0.0077384  -155.298  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughStaten Island -0.5629695  0.0095152   -59.165  < 2e-16 ***
## log_pm25_scaled      -0.0010660  0.0027963    -0.381  0.70305    
## seasonSpring          0.0227140  0.0069670     3.260  0.00111 ** 
## seasonSummer         -0.2453276  0.0073125   -33.549  < 2e-16 ***
## seasonWinter         -0.0419742  0.0074484    -5.635 1.75e-08 ***
## day_of_week.L        -0.1274815  0.0067084   -19.003  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week.Q        -0.1411564  0.0067345   -20.960  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week.C         0.0754501  0.0066451    11.354  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week^4        -0.0784216  0.0065941   -11.893  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week^5         0.0321512  0.0065942     4.876 1.08e-06 ***
## day_of_week^6        -0.0183202  0.0065916    -2.779  0.00545 ** 
## year2017             -0.0529122  0.0070806    -7.473 7.85e-14 ***
## year2018              0.0005604  0.0070450     0.080  0.93660    
## year2019             -0.0359561  0.0070994    -5.065 4.09e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for Negative Binomial(44.5407) family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 36394.7  on 7238  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  7567.4  on 7217  degrees of freedom
##   (26 observations deleted due to missingness)
## AIC: 53193
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 1
## 
## 
##               Theta:  44.54 
##           Std. Err.:  1.46 
## 
##  2 x log-likelihood:  -53147.17

Category Threshold Model

Only light and moderate rain are significant, whereas heavy rain was not significant.

8. Storm Events Analysis

I am adding storm event date and type data from the NOAA Storm Events database.

noaa_storms <- read_csv("datasets/noaa_storm_data.csv") |> 
  janitor::clean_names() |> 
  filter(event_type %in% c(
    "Heavy Rain", 
    "Flash Flood", 
    "Flood", 
    "Tropical Storm", 
    "Tropical Depression",
    "Coastal Flood"
  )) |>  # standardize all borough names
   mutate(borough = case_when(
    str_detect(cz_name_str, regex("BRONX", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Bronx",
    str_detect(cz_name_str, regex("KINGS|BROOKLYN", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Brooklyn",
    str_detect(cz_name_str, regex("NEW YORK|MANHATTAN", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Manhattan",
    str_detect(cz_name_str, regex("QUEENS", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Queens",
    str_detect(cz_name_str, regex("RICHMOND|STATEN", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Staten Island",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))
## Rows: 315 Columns: 39
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (18): CZ_NAME_STR, BEGIN_LOCATION, BEGIN_DATE, EVENT_TYPE, TOR_F_SCALE, ...
## dbl (21): EVENT_ID, BEGIN_TIME, MAGNITUDE, DEATHS_DIRECT, INJURIES_DIRECT, D...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
# convert begin and end dates to date class
noaa_storms <- noaa_storms |> 
  mutate(
  begin_date = mdy(begin_date), 
  end_date = mdy(end_date)
) |> 
  # expand each storm over its date range and borough
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(date = list(seq.Date(begin_date, end_date, by = "day"))) |> 
  unnest(date) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  select(borough, event_type, date, everything())

Merge and create lag after storm type.

storm_events <- noaa_storms |> 
  select(borough, date, event_type) |> 
  group_by(borough, date) |> 
  summarize(
     storm_event = paste(unique(event_type), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# merge with asthma_precip_pm25
asthma_model_df <- asthma_precip_pm25 |> 
  left_join(storm_events |> select(borough, date, storm_event), by = c("borough", "date")) |> 
   mutate(
    storm_event = if_else(is.na(storm_event), 0, 1)
   )

# create lags for storm events
asthma_model_df <- asthma_model_df |> 
  arrange(borough, date) |> 
  group_by(borough) |> 
  mutate(
    post_storm1 = lag(storm_event, 1, default = 0),
    post_storm2 = lag(storm_event, 2, default = 0),
    post_storm3 = lag(storm_event, 3, default = 0),
    storm_cum3  = storm_event + post_storm1 + post_storm2
  ) |> 
  ungroup()

# convert to factors
asthma_model_df <- asthma_model_df |> 
  mutate(
    storm_event = factor(storm_event, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("None","Storm")),
    post_storm1 = factor(post_storm1, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("None","PostStorm1")),
    post_storm2 = factor(post_storm2, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("None","PostStorm2")),
    post_storm3 = factor(post_storm3, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("None","PostStorm3"))
  )

# run model with storm lag
model.storm.lag <- glm.nb(
  asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled +
    storm_event + post_storm1 + post_storm2 +
    borough + season + day_of_week + year +
    offset(log(population)),
  data = asthma_model_df
)

summary(model.storm.lag)
## 
## Call:
## glm.nb(formula = asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled + 
##     storm_event + post_storm1 + post_storm2 + borough + season + 
##     day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)), data = asthma_model_df, 
##     init.theta = 44.56845745, link = log)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                         Estimate Std. Error   z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)           -9.8000389  0.0079339 -1235.207  < 2e-16 ***
## log_ppt_scaled        -0.0068234  0.0026560    -2.569  0.01020 *  
## log_pm25_scaled       -0.0011570  0.0027812    -0.416  0.67742    
## storm_eventStorm      -0.0679647  0.0330498    -2.056  0.03974 *  
## post_storm1PostStorm1 -0.0211267  0.0332221    -0.636  0.52483    
## post_storm2PostStorm2  0.0174853  0.0322920     0.541  0.58818    
## boroughBrooklyn       -0.3789052  0.0069542   -54.485  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughManhattan      -0.6081501  0.0075061   -81.021  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughQueens         -1.2010354  0.0077565  -154.843  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughStaten Island  -0.5632010  0.0095138   -59.198  < 2e-16 ***
## seasonSpring           0.0218934  0.0069427     3.153  0.00161 ** 
## seasonSummer          -0.2454687  0.0072965   -33.642  < 2e-16 ***
## seasonWinter          -0.0428110  0.0074444    -5.751 8.89e-09 ***
## day_of_week.L         -0.1275009  0.0066975   -19.037  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week.Q         -0.1413918  0.0067314   -21.005  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week.C          0.0754894  0.0066430    11.364  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week^4         -0.0790520  0.0065869   -12.001  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week^5          0.0312550  0.0065828     4.748 2.05e-06 ***
## day_of_week^6         -0.0183682  0.0065900    -2.787  0.00531 ** 
## year2017              -0.0529516  0.0070774    -7.482 7.33e-14 ***
## year2018               0.0009114  0.0070461     0.129  0.89708    
## year2019              -0.0357798  0.0070972    -5.041 4.62e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for Negative Binomial(44.5685) family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 36406.9  on 7238  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  7568.6  on 7217  degrees of freedom
##   (26 observations deleted due to missingness)
## AIC: 53192
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 1
## 
## 
##               Theta:  44.57 
##           Std. Err.:  1.46 
## 
##  2 x log-likelihood:  -53146.16

On storm days, asthma ER visits are significantly lower. There is a 6.6% decrease in asthma ER visits on storm days. The lagged effects a day after the storm (post_storm1) are not significant (p = 0.525), and we see the same story two days after a storm (post_storm2, p = 0.589).

Quantifying cumulative exposure over 3 days post-storm.

mod.storm.cumulative <- glm.nb(
  asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled + storm_cum3 +
    borough + season + day_of_week + year +
    offset(log(population)),
  data = asthma_model_df
)

summary(mod.storm.cumulative)
## 
## Call:
## glm.nb(formula = asthma_er_count ~ log_ppt_scaled + log_pm25_scaled + 
##     storm_cum3 + borough + season + day_of_week + year + offset(log(population)), 
##     data = asthma_model_df, init.theta = 44.52157943, link = log)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                        Estimate Std. Error   z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)          -9.8001018  0.0079358 -1234.918  < 2e-16 ***
## log_ppt_scaled       -0.0070896  0.0026367    -2.689  0.00717 ** 
## log_pm25_scaled      -0.0011990  0.0027813    -0.431  0.66639    
## storm_cum3           -0.0230575  0.0177901    -1.296  0.19495    
## boroughBrooklyn      -0.3789112  0.0069566   -54.468  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughManhattan     -0.6081372  0.0075083   -80.995  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughQueens        -1.2010404  0.0077585  -154.803  < 2e-16 ***
## boroughStaten Island -0.5632058  0.0095155   -59.188  < 2e-16 ***
## seasonSpring          0.0219352  0.0069445     3.159  0.00159 ** 
## seasonSummer         -0.2454053  0.0072981   -33.626  < 2e-16 ***
## seasonWinter         -0.0427166  0.0074459    -5.737 9.64e-09 ***
## day_of_week.L        -0.1274800  0.0066994   -19.029  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week.Q        -0.1415458  0.0067327   -21.024  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week.C         0.0752592  0.0066435    11.328  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week^4        -0.0789363  0.0065879   -11.982  < 2e-16 ***
## day_of_week^5         0.0313782  0.0065844     4.766 1.88e-06 ***
## day_of_week^6        -0.0183365  0.0065911    -2.782  0.00540 ** 
## year2017             -0.0529569  0.0070794    -7.480 7.41e-14 ***
## year2018              0.0009493  0.0070478     0.135  0.89285    
## year2019             -0.0357730  0.0070991    -5.039 4.68e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for Negative Binomial(44.5216) family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 36386.3  on 7238  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  7568.1  on 7219  degrees of freedom
##   (26 observations deleted due to missingness)
## AIC: 53191
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 1
## 
## 
##               Theta:  44.52 
##           Std. Err.:  1.46 
## 
##  2 x log-likelihood:  -53149.46

Model Comparison Table

model_comparison <- data.frame(
  Model = c(
    "1. Poisson (baseline)",
    "2. NB Fixed Intercepts (primary)",
    "3. NB + Borough Interaction",
    "4. NB Categorical Precipitation",
    "5. NB + Storm Events"
  ),
  AIC = c(
    AIC(pois.model),
    AIC(mod.fixed),
    AIC(mod.full),
    AIC(model.cat),
    AIC(model.storm.lag)
  ),
  Df = c(
    pois.model$df.residual,
    mod.fixed$df.residual,
    mod.full$df.residual,
    model.cat$df.residual,
    model.storm.lag$df.residual
  ),
  Notes = c(
    "Overdispersion confirmed, NB preferred",
    "Primary model",
    "LRT p=0.62, interaction not significant",
    "Sensitivity analysis",
    "Storm day effect: -6.6%, lags not significant"
  )
)

model_comparison |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(
    Model = "Model",
    AIC   = "AIC",
    Df    = "Residual df",
    Notes = "Notes"
  ) |>
  fmt_number(columns = AIC, decimals = 1) |>
  tab_header(title = "Table 3. Model Comparison Summary") |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_fill(color = "lightblue"),
    locations = cells_body(rows = 2)  # highlight primary model
  )
Table 3. Model Comparison Summary
Model AIC Residual df Notes
1. Poisson (baseline) 56,207.3 7220 Overdispersion confirmed, NB preferred
2. NB Fixed Intercepts (primary) 53,191.1 7220 Primary model
3. NB + Borough Interaction 53,191.1 7220 LRT p=0.62, interaction not significant
4. NB Categorical Precipitation 53,193.2 7217 Sensitivity analysis
5. NB + Storm Events 53,192.2 7217 Storm day effect: -6.6%, lags not significant

Time series plot of asthma rates with storm events indicated

# get unique storm event dates
storm_dates <- asthma_model_df |>
  filter(storm_event == "Storm") |>
  pull(date) |>
  unique()

# time series plot
ggplot(asthma_model_df, aes(x = date, y = asthma_rate, color = borough)) +
  # vertical lines = storm events
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = storm_dates,
    color      = "darkblue",
    linewidth  = 0.3,
    alpha      = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method  = "loess",
    span    = 0.15,
    se      = FALSE,
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Bronx"         = "#E63946",
    "Brooklyn"      = "#F4A261",
    "Manhattan"     = "#2A9D8F",
    "Queens"        = "#3B528B",
    "Staten Island" = "#8B5E3C"
  )) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "6 months",
    date_labels = "%b %Y"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ borough, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title    = "Daily Asthma ED Visit Rates by Borough, 2016–2019",
    subtitle = "Dark blue vertical lines indicate storm event days (heavy rain, flood, tropical storm, or coastal flood)",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Asthma ED Visits per 100,000",
    color    = "Borough"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 9, color = "#555555", margin = margin(b = 8)),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# save
ggsave(
  filename = "outputs/timeseries_asthma_storm_events.png",
  plot     = last_plot(),
  width    = 10,
  height   = 12,
  dpi      = 300
)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
